subtasks.description: Query SQL warehouse to identify top spending publishers and their total spend amounts, ranked by spend volume
Query MongoDB to retrieve product portfolio and product line breakdown for each of the identified top publishers

Reconcile publisher spend data with product mix, calculate negotiation leverage metrics (concentration, growth trends, competitive alternatives), and generate leverage assessment and negotiation recommendations for each publisher

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks required: (1) SQL query to identify top publishers by spend (small tier—straightforward ranking); (2) MongoDB query to fetch product mix for those publishers (small tier—standard document retrieval); (3) Cross-reconciliation (mid tier) to merge spend + product data, calculate leverage factors (concentration ratio, exclusivity, competitive alternatives), and generate negotiation posture/recommendations. The leverage assessment is a reconciliation + decision task (not a separate policy_action), as it synthesizes two data sources and applies business logic. Mid tier assigned for cross_recon due to multi-signal reasoning (spend, product concentration, competitive landscape integration).

output.sql: SELECT publisher, SUM(amount_usd) as total_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT product) as product_count, ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT product) as products FROM consumption_facts WHERE amount_usd > 0 GROUP BY publisher ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 20
